prompt:
I just had a 13 minute Google Meet call and the recording sounds weird - speakers voices sound slightly doubled/echoed. Can you check the quality of this recording and figure out what's going on?

The recording is at: /Users/tenequm/Library/Application Support/Blackbox/Recordings/2026-03-12-140342-A8DF

assertions.text: Ran the bundled analyze_recording.py script (or equivalent comprehensive analysis) on the recording
Identified echo/duplication in the system audio track (not just mic) via autocorrelation or similar method
Reported the approximate echo lag (30-53ms range) from the autocorrelation peaks
Noted that cross-track correlation is near zero (the echo is NOT mic bleed from speakers)
Flagged that AEC-processed tracks are over-amplified (too loud)
Reported PESQ and/or STOI scores showing AEC processing quality
Provided an actionable explanation of what's causing the echo (virtual audio processor, Krisp, or similar hypothesis)
